title: cerastium gibraltaricum var. viridulum
intro: Cerastium gibraltaricum var. viridulum, also known as the Gibraltar mouse-ear chickweed, is a plant species in the carnation family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor infections. The health benefits of Cerastium gibraltaricum var. viridulum include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
